agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "SHIP WITH FIXES",
    "one_liner": "Off-by-one pagination bug skips first page of results; missing input validation allows SQL injection of negative OFFSET/LIMIT"
  },

  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "Off-by-one error: page 1 skips the first `pageSize` rows",
      "risk": "Users requesting page 1 (the documented first page) get `OFFSET = 1 * pageSize`, skipping the most recent orders entirely. Page 0 returns the real first page but is undocumented. Every customer sees wrong data.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "getOrderHistory, line: `const offset = page * pageSize`",
      "reproduction": "Call `getOrderHistory('c1', 1, 10)`. Offset is `1 * 10 = 10`, so the 10 newest orders are silently skipped. The caller believes they see the first page but they see the second.",
      "fix": "Change to `const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize;` to align with the 1-indexed API contract.",
      "time_to_fix": "< 5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "API contract states page=1 is the first page; current implementation violates this"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on `page` and `pageSize` — negative/zero values produce nonsensical or dangerous queries",
      "risk": "A caller can pass `page=0`, `page=-1`, or `pageSize=-1`. Negative OFFSET behavior is database-dependent (Postgres treats negative OFFSET as 0, but negative LIMIT throws an error). `pageSize=0` returns nothing. `pageSize=999999` dumps the entire table, enabling a denial-of-service or data-exfiltration vector.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrderHistory, function signature / entry",
      "reproduction": "Call `getOrderHistory('c1', -1, 50)` — offset becomes negative. Call `getOrderHistory('c1', 1, 1000000)` — returns unbounded rows, potential OOM or slow query.",
      "fix": "Add guard: `if (page < 1) throw new Error('page must be >= 1'); if (pageSize < 1 || pageSize > MAX_PAGE_SIZE) throw new Error('pageSize out of range');` with a sensible MAX_PAGE_SIZE (e.g., 100).",
      "time_to_fix": "< 10 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "API stability and resource protection"
    },
    {
      "title": "No total count or next-page signal — clients cannot detect end of results",
      "risk": "The function returns `Order[]` with no total count or `hasNextPage` indicator. Clients either poll forever (requesting page N+1 and getting an empty array each time) or must guess when to stop. This also means the UI cannot render 'Page X of Y'. Under OFFSET pagination, the DB still scans all skipped rows, so deep pages degrade linearly — a customer with 100k orders requesting page 1000 forces Postgres to scan and discard 999 * pageSize rows.",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "getOrderHistory return type and query",
      "reproduction": "Request progressively higher page numbers — response time grows linearly. No signal tells the client to stop.",
      "fix": "Return `{ orders: Order[], totalCount: number }` (add a `SELECT COUNT(*)` or use a window function `COUNT(*) OVER()`), or switch to cursor-based pagination (`WHERE created_at < $lastSeen`) for large datasets.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes – 1 hour",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Usability and performance at scale"
    }
  ],

  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "`SELECT *` returns all columns including potentially large/sensitive fields",
      "description": "Using `SELECT *` pulls every column (e.g., internal notes, raw payment tokens) into memory. If the schema grows, this silently returns more data than the API contract promises, increasing payload size and risking data leakage.",
      "location": "SQL query"
    },
    {
      "title": "No index guarantee on `(customer_id, created_at DESC)`",
      "description": "The query filters on `customer_id` and sorts on `created_at DESC`. Without a composite index, this becomes a sequential scan + sort for customers with many orders. Should verify a covering index exists.",
      "location": "SQL query WHERE/ORDER BY"
    }
  ],

  "suggestions": [
    "Add an explicit return type wrapper (e.g., `PaginatedResponse<Order>`) that includes `page`, `pageSize`, `totalCount`, and `hasNextPage` to formalize the pagination contract.",
    "Consider cursor-based pagination (`WHERE created_at < :cursor ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT :pageSize`) if order volumes are high — OFFSET pagination degrades at depth.",
    "Validate `customerId` is non-empty to prevent a full-table scan on `WHERE customer_id = ''`."
  ],

  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "The off-by-one bug means every existing consumer on page=1 is silently getting wrong data. Fixing it changes which rows are returned, so any client that cached or compensated for the bug will break. Coordinate fix with a version bump or client notification.",
    "rollback_capability": "Safe to roll back — the fix is a pure computation change with no state or migration.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fixing the offset changes the data returned for every page number. If clients have adapted to the broken behavior, the fix is itself a breaking change. Consider whether any downstream caches or saved page references exist.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring mentioned. Add logging/metrics for: requests with out-of-range page/pageSize, queries exceeding a latency threshold, empty result sets (signal that client is paginating past the end).",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": true,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": ["Fixing the off-by-one changes which rows are returned for every page value, which is a behavioral API break for existing consumers."]
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Revert the offset calculation to `page * pageSize` if downstream breakage is detected. No migration or data changes involved."
  },

  "escalations": [],

  "confidence": {
    "level": 95,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Off-by-one bug is deterministic and provable from the code + API doc",
      "Missing input validation is directly observable",
      "OFFSET pagination performance characteristics are well-known"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether a composite index on (customer_id, created_at) exists — would need to inspect the schema"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The database is PostgreSQL (based on $1/$2/$3 parameterized query syntax)",
      "The API documentation's 1-indexed contract is authoritative"
    ]
  }
}
